texts.full_text: on his body stood on end as he unraveled. Her touch gently coerced the stress out of his body, slowly, making him believe that they were okay. He released her warm body for a quick split second just so that he could capture her lips as insurance for her claim. She was startled by his desperation; each tug of his lips spoke volumes about his distress. He narrated his need for her through his actions and she responded with the very same passion. With an unmistaken sense of urgency, he pulled her body into his own and unknowcompletion: of what had been driving him since their separation: a deep fear that this would be nothing but another ghostly fantasy. "Remember our promise?" Her question was gentle yet held the weightiness it always did. "To never let go of each other," he reminded her with an uplifting resolve, as though holding onto those words alone could prevent any further loss they might face together or apart in life's uncertain paths. As their lips met once more and a tender embrace became intertwined again through the unspoken bond that existed beyond time – it was clear. They were no longer standing on separate worlds; rather, two pieces of one soul had rejoiced back into each other’s arms. The day around them faded out as if to make space for their private moment in eternity's shadowy embrace. "Now and forever," they whispered the words against every tender touch which spoke louder than any spoken vow could ever have. And though surrounded by uncertainty, an unspoken pact was sealed that no pain or separation would be enough

texts.full_text: Adam Blagojevich defense team almost set May 18, 2009 Sam Adam Jr. formally filed his appearance today to represent Gov. Rod Blagojevich in his federal corruption case, and his father, Sam Adam, is expected to soon join him. They will team up with attorney Sheldon Sorosky in the former governor's defense.The roles the Adams will play were cemented in meetings late week after U.S. District Judge James Zagel informed Blagojevich's defense that he would allow three attorneys to be paid from more than $2 million that remains in the former governor's campaign fund. However, they can be paid at only the rate of lawyers, far below the usual fee of trial attorneys. Zagel said a fourth lawyer might be added as well but only for the purpose of processing evidence in the case and not for trial. The rate limitation is a point of contention, as the defense team is accustomed to a much higher rate for their expertise and the case's complexity. The decision to allow only a portion of the defense team to be funded in this manner is a significant limitation, and the defense is currently navigating the financial and strategic implications of this decision. The team is expected to focus their efforts on a robust defense, navigating the challenges of the case and the financial constraints imposed. The involvement of the Adams and their strategic decision to work on this case, despite the financial constraints, is a testament to their commitment to their client and their belief in the case. Names of gitweb features are keys of this hash.</simpara> <simpara>Each <emphasis>%feature</emphasis> hash element is a hash reference and has the following structure:</simpara> <screen>"<feature_name>" => { "sub" => <feature-sub (subroutine)>, "override" => <allow-override (boolean)>, "default" => [ <options>... ] },</screen> <simpara>Some features cannot be overridden per project. For those features the structure of appropriate <emphasis>%feature</emphasis> hash element has a simpler form:</simpara> <screen>"<feature_name>" => { "override" => 0, "default" => [ <options>... ] },</screen> <simpara>As one can see it lacks the 'sub' element.</simpara> <simpara>The meaning of each part of feature configuration is described below:</simpara> <variablelist> <varlistentry> <term> default </term> <listitem> <simpara> List (array reference) of feature parameters (if there are any), used also to toggle (enable or disable) given feature. </simpara> <simpara>Note that it is currently <emphasis role="strong">always</emphasis> an array reference, even if feature doesn't accept any configuration parameters, and 'default' is used only to turn it on or off. In such case you turn feature on by setting this element to <emphasis>[1]</emphasis>, and torn it off by setting it to <emphasis>[0]</emphasis>. See also the passage about the "blame" feature in the "Examples" section.</simpara> <simpara>To disable features that accept parameters (are configurable), you need to set this element to empty list i.e. <emphasis>[]</emphasis>.</simpara> </listitem> </varlistentry> <varlistentry> <term> override </term>

texts.full_text: arol Myers-Scotton (born 1934) is an American linguist. She was a Distinguished Professor Emerita in the Linguistics Program and Department of English at the University of South Carolina until 2003. Education She received her A.B. from Grinnell College in 1955, and her M.A. in English in 1961 and Ph.D. in linguistics in 1967, both from the University of Wisconsin–Madison. Career She resided in Columbia, South Carolina until 2003, where she was Carolina Distinguished Professor at the University of South Carolina in the Linguistics Program and Department of English. She currently resides in Michigan, where she is an adjunct professor in the Department of Linguistics and Languages at Michigan State University, and also a visiting scholar at the MSU African StudiCenter. Notable Work Myers-Scotton's work includes her code-switching theory known as "Markedness Model," which argues that speakers switch languages based on factors rather than linguistic ones. Her book titled Motivations for Code-Switching'" (1983) and '"Language Choice: Codes Switching in Bilinguals"'(2006), both published by Oxford University Press, are considered seminal works in the field of linguistics. Awards Myers-Scotton has received numerous awards throughout her career including: Guggenheim Fellowship for Natural Sciences and Mathematics/Physics/Paleontology/Chemistry/Biology/Linguistics/Mathematics/Astronomy/(Neuroscience) category in 2004 * Distinguished Service to the Profession of Linguistics award from The American Association for Applied Linguistics (AAAL) Legacy Carol Myers-Scotton's work has significantly contributed to our

At the beginning of the marketplace competition there were five competing systems. Over the eight years the number of competitors has been reduced to two. Check for Obstructions: Carefully disassemble parts where paper could be stuck, focusing particularly around any moving sections like rOllers and input/output trays. 2. Clean Sensors: Use a lint-free cloth slightly dampened with isopropyl alcohol to clean the optical sensors inside your Canon MP160. 3. Reset Printer Settings: - Turn off printer completely (not just standby). - Unplug power cord, wait 5 minutes. - Reconnect and turn on again; check for any prompts or error messages during startup which might guide you

texts.full_text: s closed. Using a `Service` is indeed a good approach for this as it allows your code to run in the background. Here's how you can complete the `onLocationChanged` method within your `GpsService`: ```java public void onLocationChanged(Location loc) { // This method will be called whenever a new location is available // You can perform actions with the new location data here, such as updating a database or UI elements // For example: double latitude = loc.getLatitude(); double longitude = loc.getLongitude(); // Do something with the latitude and longitude } ``` In your main application class (the one that extends `Application`), you can start this service in the `onCreate` method like so: ```java public class MyApp extends Application { @Override public void onCreate() { super.onCreate(); Intent gpsServiceIntent = new Intent(this, GpsService.class); startService(gpsServiceIntent); } } ``` This ensures that your GPS location updates begin as soon as the application is initialized and will continue to run even if the app is closed or sent to the background. texts.full_text: on the radiation-reaction timescale (like $J$ and the precession morphology) will remain robust down to small separations, as explored in Secs. \[msasymmetry\] and \[binmemory\] below. ![CPU time needed to evolve BBHs from an initial separation $r_i$ to a final separation $r_f=10M$ using our new precession-averaged approach (purple circles) and the standard orbit-averaged approach (orange triangles). Each CPU time is averaged over $N=100$ executions with isotropicinitial spin orientation (flat distributions in $\cos\theta_{1}$, $\cos\theta_{2}$ and $\Delta\Phi$). Dashed lines show the expected scalings: $t\propto r_i^{3/2}$ for the orbit-averaged approach and $t\propto \log r_i$ for our new precession-averaged approach.These computations have been performed on a single core of a 2013 Intel i5-3470 3.20GHz CPU.[]{data-label="timing"}](timing){width="\columnwidth"} We compare the computational efficiency of the precession- and orbit-averaged approaches in Fig. \[timing\]. Isotropic samples of 100 BBHs are transferred from large initial separations $r_i$ to a final separation $r_f=10M$. The CPU time required by the two approaches scalesdifferently with the initial separation. The orbit-averaged (OA) equations must be integrated with a time step shorter than the precession time, implying that the total number of time steps scales as $$\begin{aligned} N_{\rm OA}\propto \int_{r_{f}}^{r_{i}} \frac{dr}{ \dot r_{GW} \;t_{\rm pre}}\sim r_i^{3/2}\,,\end{aligned}$$ where $\dot r_{GW}\propto r^{-3}$ as given by the quadrupole formula [@1963PhRv..131..435P; @1964PhRv..136.1224P]. The ratio $t_{\rm RR}/t_{\rm pre} \propto r^{3/2}$ increases dramatically at large separations leading to a corresponding increase in the computational cost. In the precession-averaged (PA) approach,the integration of $dJ/dL$ in Eq. (\[ourODE\]) only requires time steps proportional to $L$, hence $$\begin{aligned} N_{\rm PA} \propto \int^{L_i}_{L_f} \frac{dL}{L} \sim \log(L_i) \propto \log(r_i)\,.\end{aligned}$$ The precession-averaged approach is very efficient at large separations because the solutions to Eq. (\[ourODE\]) become very smooth in this limit as seen from Eq. (\[Jlarge\]) and Fig. \[Jflow\]. Precession-averaged inspirals may even be computed frominfiniteseparations through a changeof variables to $u \equiv (2L)^{-1}$. A seven-step monitoring and evaluation (M&E) process was adapted for this study that combined different steps within a logical framework approach. A retrospective analysis found that M&E is not part of the HA program (HAP) process from nomination through completion and beyond. texts.full_text: e you to label me a chauvinist. But I have sat through the entire made-for TV/DVD Love series by Janette Oke and my conclusion is that the screen adaptations are more suited to feminine sensibilities. This is a chick flick of the highest order. So, what’s wrong with that? Guys are bombarded by movies that go boom, so why shouldn’t the fairer sex have sentimental movies about lifting up what we should all hold dear: love, honor, and regard for others and reverence for God and matters of faith. Melodramatic, yes; set on a dustless frontier, for sure; but the production, like its predecessors, is involving, gently paced, and after several doses of tame theatrics, leaves viewers with a happy ending. Come to think of it, I may watch it again. Not rated, I found nothing objectionable. It’s a soap opera set in the Old West,

texts.full_text: an unincorporated community in Unicoi County, Tex nnsylvania. As of the 2010 census, there were no incorporated communities with this name or similar variations recognized by local governments. The location referred to as "Clearbranch" may be a residential area within larger municipalities such as Erwin, Tennessee (population: 5,593), which is located about five miles west of Clearbranch. There are places nearby that share the term 'clear branch' in their names; for instance, Clear Branch Creek flows through Unicoi County. The creek's name comes from its transparent waters due to being spring-fed and not containing any sediment or debris like other water bodies do which results in a very clear flow of water.
